original: Dedication of the Cross Group temples (Temples of the Cross, Foliated Cross and Sun) at Maya city of Palenque
Lotario di Segni elected Pope Innocentius III
Earl Ferrand of Flanders drops ties with France
Monaco gains independence
The Great Frost: in London the Thames river froze so thickly bonfires were lit on it
Pope Eugene IV tries to break the power of the Council of Basel by transferring it to Ferrara
French troops under Duc de Guise occupy Calais
Jews are expelled from Genoa, Italy
Oldest surviving commercial newspaper, "Weeckelycke Courante van Europa", now known as "Haarlems Dagblad", still in printed circulation begins publication in Haarlem, Netherlands
First American commercial corporation chartered (NY Fishing Co.)
Spanish Armada headed by the San José and loaded with gold, sinks after a British squadron attacks off the coast of Colombia (rediscovered in 2015)
Amsterdam gang leader and thief "Sjako" arrested
Premiere ofGeorge Frideric Handel's opera "Ariodante" at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden, London
Britain, Austria, Netherlands & Saxony sign anti-Prussian Quadruple Alliance
Comet C/1760 A1 (Great comet) approaches within 0.0682 AUs of Earth
11th Amendment ratified, judicial powers construed
Austrians defeat French in Second Battle of Novi
Wild Boy of Aveyron (approx.12) discovered in southern France after possibly 7 years in the wild, later christened Victor of Aveyron
Battle of Blaauwberg: British forces attack French vassal, the Batavian Republic near Cape Town, modern day South Africa
Louisiana slave revolt by Charles Deslondes at German Coast
Battle of New Orleans (War of 1812); the war had ended on 24th December 1814 but none of the combatants knew
Boston Academy of Music, 1st US music school, established
US national debt is $0 for the first and only time in history
Rebellion at Amherstburg, Ontario breaks out
Dutch King Willem II charters Technical College Delft
Austrian soldiers kill 10 students, Pavia
First US bronze equestrian statue (of Andrew Jackson) is unveiled in Washington, D.C.
Dr John Veatch discovers large disports of borax at Tuscan Springs, California
Dion Boucicauly's "Poor of NY" premieres in NYC
US Civil War: Union forces abandon Fort Barrancas in favor of Fort Pikens on Santa Rosa Island, near Pensacola, Florida
American Civil War: Second Battle of Springfield
US mint at Carson City, Nevada begins issuing coins
Chrome tanning process for leather patented by Augustus Schultz
Dr Herman Hollerith receives 1st US patent for a mechanical tabulating machine
Columbus World's fair in Chicago destroyed by fire
Michael Eagan wins 1st US national amateur handball championship
1st National Bowling Championship held (Chicago, Illinois)
The United Irish League, a leading force for unification in all Ireland and independence from Britain, holds its convention in Dublin
Pope Pius X banned low cut dresses in the presence of churchmen
Landslide kills 20 people in Haverstraw, New York, caused by the excavation of clay along the Hudson River
First subway tunnel connecting Manhattan with Brooklyn under the East River opens in New York City

Chiefs, representatives of people's and church organizations, and other prominent individuals form the African National Congress and declare its aim to bring all Africans together as one people to defend their rights and freedoms
Austria-Hungarian troops conquer Forlani Italy
Romanian conductorGeorge Enescumakes his American debut, leading the Philadelphia Orchestra at Carnegie Hall in New York City
Typography strike in Amsterdam
England cricket opening batsman Herbert Sutcliffe follows his 1st innings 176 with 127 in the 2nd innings but his team cannot avoid an 81 run defeat to Australia in the 2nd Test in Melbourne
First all-female US state supreme court appointed in Texas
"Man with a Movie Camera", Soviet experimental documentary film directed by Dziga Vertov, is released
CBS radio network buys WABC in NYC
First telephone connection between the Netherlands and the West Indies
Ratification of present San Francisco City Charter
Jaap Speyers "Bluejackets" premieres in Amsterdam
Spectrophotometer patented, AC Hardy
-50°F (-45.6°C), San Jacinto, Nevada (state record)
Britain's first World War II rationing includes bacon, butter, and sugar
British Air Marshal Richard Peirse replaced as Air Officer Commanding-in-Chief Bomber Command
"Youth for Christ" organizes
University of Kentucky Wildcats men's basketball team sets an NCAA record by holding Arkansas State to 6 points in a 75-6 win
General George Marshall becomes US Secretary of State
Toronto Maple Leaf rookie Howie Meeker scores 5 goals in a game
Jordan adopts constitution
Indians bar night games with Browns (who refuse to share TV receipts)
René Mayer forms French government
Furman sets NCAA basketball single-game scoring record with 154 pt
Georgia Tech ends Kentucky's 130-game home basketball win streak
WUNC TV channel 4 in Chapel Hill, NC (PBS) begins broadcasting
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to Conrad Aiken
Operation Auca: Five U.S. missionaries are killed by the Huaorani of Ecuador shortly after making contact with them.
Bollingen Prize for Poetry is awarded to Yvor Winters
The French vote for Algerian independence from French rule in the wake of seven years of guerrilla war
Dutch express train crashes into a slow commuter train, killing 93 people
"Mona Lisa", on loan, unveiled in America's National Gallery of Art
Senator Everett Dirksen introduces a bill to make marigold the American natonal flower (it does not pass)
Star of India returned to American Museum of Natural History
British rock bands The Who and The Kinks perform on the last "Shindig" TV show on ABC
The Beatles' "Rubber Soul" album goes to #1 and stays #1 for six weeks, while the single "We Can Work It Out" also charts at #1 for three weeks
29 pilot whales beach themselves & die at San Clemente Island, California
Voyageurs National Park, Minn, established
Dmitri Shostakovich' 15th Symphony premieres in Moscow
NCAA announces freshman can play on teams starting in fall
Judge Sirica begins the trial of the Watergate burglars in Washington, D.C.
Secret peace talks between US & North Vietnam resumed near Paris
USSR launches Luna 21 for Moon landing
Earl Wilson, Jr. and Phil Oesterman's musical "Let My People Come" premieres in NYC
Gold hits record $126.50 an ounce in London
Silver hits a record $3.40 an ounce in New York
Franklin Mint strikes 1st gold coins for Netherlands Antilles
Israeli government votes to 'strengthen' settlements in occupied Sinai
Argentina & Chile sign Beagle Canal accord
French oil tanker  MV Betelgeuse explodes while offloading its cargo in Bantry Bay, West Cork, Ireland, killing 50 crew members; a salvage diver also dies later
Today Show gets a new theme song
Vietnamese troops overtook Khmer Rouge & occupy Phnom Penh
Islander Glenn Resch's 20th shut-out opponent-Canucks 3-0
NCAA decides to sponsor women's championships in 5 sports
India all out 63 in one-day international v Australia
AT&T agrees to divest itself of 22 Bell System companies
Johnny Cash Parkway opens in Hendersonville, Tennessee
US Justice Department withdraws anti-trust suit against IBM, pending since 1969
NCAA announces that basketball tournament will have 64 teams
Washington Capitals Bengt Gustafsson scores 5 goals to beat Philadelphia 7-1
Japan launches Sakigake space probe to Halley's Comet
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 2,000 for 1st time (2,002.25)
Jack Sikma (Milwaukee) begins NBA free throw streak of 51 games
9th largest NBA crowd 38,873-Chicago at Detroit
Dow Jones Industrial Average down 140.58 points
Beginning of the Japanese Heisei era
Boeing 737-400 crashes at Kegworth, England, 46 die
Soviet Union promises to eliminate stockpiles of chemical weapons
Tamas Darnyi swims world record 400m medley (4:12.36)
Rintje Ritsma skates world record 1500m (1:51.60)
Russian manned space craft TM-18, launches into orbit
15th United Negro College Fund raises $12,200,000
Blizzard buries eastern US causing at least 50 deaths
For 1st time in 25 years no one is elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
NY Giant GM George Young resigns to accept NFL position
World Trade Center bomber Ramzi Ahmed Yousef sentenced to life in prison
"Music City Miracle", in AFC Wild Card Playoff, Tennessee Titans defeat Buffalo Bills 22-16 in last 16 seconds with Kevin Dyson, running length of the field for a game-winning touchdown
The identities of 2 boys who murdered a toddler in 1993 will be kept secret, the High Court rules
A magnitude 6.9 earthquake with its epicenter just off the Greek island of Kythira hits much of the country and is felt throughout the entire eastern Mediterranean Sea.
New Jersey officially apologizes for slavery, becoming the first Northern state to do so
A 6.2 magnitude earthquake hit Costa Rica´s region of Volcan Poás, with an epicenter near Cinchona. It was caused by Varablanca-Angel fault.
Attempted assassination of Arizona congresswoman Gabrielle Giffords and subsequent shooting in Casas Adobes, Arizona at a Safeway grocery store kills 6 and wounds 13, including Giffords

"The 3:16 Game" AFC Wild Card Playof, underdogs Denver Broncos defeat Pittsburgh Steelers 29–23 with Tim Tebow connecting with Demaryius Thomas on an 80-yard touchdown pass on first scrimmage play of overtime
130 wildfires across Australia’s east coast force thousands to evacuate their homes
2,130 prisoners held by the Syrian government are exchanged for 48 Iranians kidnapped by Syrian rebels
First known interstellar meteor to ever hit Earth - CNEOS 2014-01-08 crash lands in Papua New Guinea
Self-declared Republic of Somaliland passes its first ever law against rape
US record year for cost of natural disasters announced - $306 billion in 2017
Duke and Duchess of Sussex announce they are stepping back as "senior" royals, will work towards becoming financially independent
Iran launches missile strike on Irbil and Al Asad bases in Iraqi housing some American troops in retaliation to assassination of General Qasem Soleimani
Ukrainian Boeing 737-800 crashes just after take-off from Tehran, Iran, killing all 176 people on board
Storm Filomena hits Spain with 50cm (20 inches) of snow falling on Madrid, resulting in four deaths and leaving thousands stranded
Dan Campbell's Detroit Lions finish the season with a 9–8 record, achieving their first winning season since 2017
The United Kingdom passes 150,000 COVID-19 deaths, the first country in Europe and the seventh globally
China loosens travel restrictions and reopens its borders after nearly three years
United Cup Tennis, Ken Rosewall Arena, Sydney, Australia: US wins inaugural event, 4-0 over Italy
Electronics brand LG unveils their Signature OLED T, the world's first wireless TV with a transparent screen at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas
The decision to seizeAndrew Tate's assets is overturned by Bucharest's Court of Appeal after a successful appeal, ordering a new trial over the assets

US Army veteran Henry "Robby" Robinson (98) is awarded France's Medal of Honor for his service in WWII by French Ambassador to the US in a ceremony in Las Vegas, Nevada; Robinson worked as a scout behind enemy lines, fought in the Battle of the Bulge, participated in the liberation of the Mauthausen and Gusen concentration camps, and served as a guard during the Nuremberg Trials
Vulcan, NASA's first commercially financed moon mission launches from Cape Canaveral, Florida but its lander Peregrine develops problems soon after
Battle of Ashdown: Ethelred I of Wessex and his brotherAlfred the Greatbeat invading Danish army
German astronomer Simon Marius independently discovers the first four moons of Jupiter one day afterGalileo
George Frideric Handel's first opera "Almira" premieres in Hamburg, Germany

ExplorerWilliam Clarkviews skeleton of a 105 ft whale washed up on Cannon Beach, inhabited by the Tillamook Nation (modern Oregon)
Sophie Germainis the first woman to win a prize from the Paris Academy of Sciences for her paper on elasticity
African American men granted the right to vote in Washington, D.C. despite PresidentAndrew Johnson's veto
Crazy Horseand his Sioux warriors fight their last battle with the United States Cavalry at Wolf Mountain (Montana Territory).
Secret meeting of Belgian KingLeopold II's agent andHenry Morton Stanley
Frank Chancebecomes NY Yankees manager

AbdulazizIbn Saudbecomes King of Nejd and Hejaz, forerunner of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia

21-year-old future Hall of Fame golferJack Nicklausmakes his first professional appearance; tied 50th in Los Angeles Open

Georges Pompidoure-appointed French Prime Minister and forms new government

TV series "All Creatures Great and Small" debuts on BBC TV, based on the books byJames Herriot, starring Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

Hewlett-Packardintroduces HP-28S Advanced Scientific Calculator
"Davis Rules" withJonathan Winters& Randy Quaid premieres on ABC-TV
Gaylord Perry,Ferguson Jenkins&Rod Carewelected to Baseball Hall of Fame
George H. W. Bushfalls ill and vomits on Japanese prime minister's lap
Elvis PresleyCommemorative Postage Stamp goes on sale

Michael Jordan's game-high 35 points leads Chicago to a 120-95 win over Milwaukee, giving him exactly 20,000 points in the 620th game of his NBA career, the second-fastest to reach this milestone after Wilt Chamberlain (499)
NBC offers "The Tonight Show" toDavid Letterman
Robert Rodriguez's "El Mariachi" debuts at the Toronto Film Fetsival - will become lowest-budgeted film ever to gross $1 million
Philadelphia Phillies 12-time All Star third basemanMike Schmidtis elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
Unabomber suspectTed Kaczynskiasks to act as his own lawyer
Tree planting protest against the privatisation of Karura Forest, includingWangari Maathai, attacked in Kenya prompting outrage

Joe Gibbsretires, for the second time, as head coach of the Washington Redskins

ISO/Columbia Records/Sony Music releaseDavid Bowie's 26th and final studio album "Blackstar" on his 69th birthday; enters charts in US and UK at #1

Japan's Prime MinisterYoshihide Sugadeclares a state of emergency for Tokyo and surrounding areas after COVID-19 cases surge to their highest level
Twitter bans US PresidentDonald Trumppermanently "due to the risk of further incitement of violence"
